chunk_summary: This section discusses fluid and sodium management strategies in pediatric CKD, emphasizing individualized fluid allowances, sodium restriction, and monitoring for sodium depletion or excess to prevent complications like hypertension and growth failure.
learning_objectives: Understand fluid allowance calculations and dry weight targets in pediatric CKD.
Learn sodium restriction strategies based on age-specific guidelines and clinical conditions.
Identify signs of sodium depletion and appropriate interventions in pediatric CKD.
Recognize the role of dietary sodium sources in managing fluid and thirst in children with CKD.
key_facts: Fluid allowance in pediatric CKD includes insensible losses, urine output, and replacement for additional losses (e.g., vomiting, diarrhea).
Interdialytic weight gain up to 5% above dry weight is acceptable; higher gains indicate excess sodium/fluid intake or inadequate dialysis.
Sodium restriction should follow age-based guidelines: infants use AI ranges, older children should not exceed CDRR thresholds.
Sodium depletion requires increased intake, especially in cases of renal/extrarenal losses, hypotension, or growth failure.
Common high-sodium foods to limit include papad, achar, namkeen, instant noodles, and restaurant gravies.
reference_quote: Fluid prescriptions in pediatric CKD are safest when written as a calculation plus a monitoring target. (KDOQI Clinical Practice Guidelines for Nutrition in Children with CKD: 2008 Update)
document_key_topics: Fluid allowance and dry weight targets in pediatric CKD
Monitoring for sodium depletion and excess
Interdialytic weight gain and dialysis adherence
